variables.name: DATABASE_URL
variables.purpose: PostgreSQL connection string for the primary application database

variables.name: JWT_SECRET
variables.purpose: Secret key for signing and verifying JWT authentication tokens
variables.securityNotes: Must be a cryptographically random string with minimum 256 bits of entropy. Generate using secure random generator (e.g., openssl rand -base64 32). Never use dictionary words, predictable patterns, or short strings. Rotation recommended every 90 days for production systems.

variables.name: PORT
variables.purpose: HTTP port the server listens on

variables.name: NODE_ENV
variables.purpose: Node.js environment mode. Controls logging verbosity, error detail exposure, and development tooling

variables.name: FILE_STORAGE_BACKEND
variables.purpose: Cloud storage backend for execution file uploads and downloads. Determines which storage adapter is used at runtime

variables.name: R2_ACCOUNT_ID
variables.purpose: Cloudflare account ID for R2 object storage. Required when using Cloudflare R2 as the file storage backend

variables.name: R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID
variables.purpose: R2 API access key ID for authenticating with Cloudflare R2 storage

variables.name: R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
variables.purpose: R2 API secret access key for authenticating with Cloudflare R2 storage

variables.name: R2_BUCKET_NAME
variables.purpose: Name of the R2 bucket where execution files are stored

variables.name: S3_REGION
variables.purpose: AWS region where the S3 bucket is located

variables.name: S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID
variables.purpose: AWS IAM access key ID for authenticating with Amazon S3

variables.name: S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
variables.purpose: AWS IAM secret access key for authenticating with Amazon S3

variables.name: S3_BUCKET_NAME
variables.purpose: Name of the S3 bucket where execution files are stored

variables.name: EMAIL_PROVIDER
variables.purpose: Email delivery provider. Controls which email adapter is used for execution completion notifications and invitation emails

variables.name: EMAIL_FROM
variables.purpose: Sender email address displayed on all outgoing emails (notifications, invitations)

variables.name: SENDGRID_API_KEY
variables.purpose: SendGrid API key for sending transactional emails via the SendGrid platform

variables.name: SMTP_HOST
variables.purpose: SMTP server hostname for sending email when using the SMTP provider

variables.name: SMTP_PORT
variables.purpose: SMTP server port. Typically 587 (STARTTLS) or 465 (SSL)

variables.name: SMTP_USER
variables.purpose: Username for authenticating with the SMTP server

variables.name: SMTP_PASS
variables.purpose: Password for authenticating with the SMTP server

variables.name: JOB_QUEUE_BACKEND
variables.purpose: Background job queue backend engine. Controls whether pg-boss (PostgreSQL-backed, no extra infrastructure) or BullMQ (Redis-backed, higher throughput) is used for pipeline execution.

variables.name: REDIS_URL
variables.purpose: Redis connection string for the BullMQ job queue backend. Only required when JOB_QUEUE_BACKEND is set to bullmq.

variables.name: QUEUE_CONCURRENCY
variables.purpose: Number of executions processed concurrently by queue workers. Higher values increase throughput but place more load on workflow engines

variables.name: FILE_RETENTION_DAYS
variables.purpose: Number of days execution files are retained before expiry and deletion from cloud storage

variables.name: EXECUTION_TIMEOUT_DEFAULT_SECONDS
variables.purpose: Default execution timeout in seconds applied to new tasks if not explicitly configured on the task. Can be overridden per task.

variables.name: LOG_LEVEL
variables.purpose: Application log verbosity level

variables.name: CORS_ORIGINS
variables.purpose: Comma-separated list of allowed CORS origins for the API. Restrict to your frontend domain in production.

variables.name: INVITE_TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS
variables.purpose: Number of hours an email invitation link remains valid before expiring
